# Pinned dependencies for reproducibility.
# Target: Python 3.11, linux/amd64 (Docker) — also installs natively on Windows/WSL2.
#
# These top-level pins resolve to a conflict-free set (verified: both PennyLane
# backends import + instantiate). The FULL transitive lock is in requirements.lock.txt
# (generated by `pip freeze` inside the built image) — install that for byte-identical envs.
#
# Compatibility note: PennyLane >=0.41 uses autoray's modern API (get_namespace);
# autoray 0.8.x is therefore required for quimb/cotengra AND PennyLane to coexist.
# (PennyLane 0.39 + quimb is NOT co-installable: it needs autoray <0.8 which breaks cotengra.)

# --- core numerics ---
numpy==2.4.6
scipy==1.17.1

# --- quantum circuits (PennyLane stack) ---
# lightning.qubit  -> dense statevector backend for n <= ~16
# default.tensor   -> quimb MPS backend for n = 32
pennylane==0.45.0
pennylane-lightning==0.45.0

# --- tensor-network / DMRG ground-state generation ---
quimb==1.14.0
autoray==0.8.4
cotengra==0.8.1
opt-einsum==3.4.0

# --- optimizer (gradient-free, as in the paper) ---
cma==4.4.4

# --- config / IO / plotting / tooling ---
pyyaml==6.0.3
pandas==3.0.3
matplotlib==3.10.9
tqdm==4.68.2

# --- tests ---
pytest==9.0.3
